meta_title: For Founders: debugging with AI to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Debugging with AI to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on 'no project ideas.' Learn how to use AI debugging to uncover profitable SaaS opportunities with this expert-led, 5-step founder checklist.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for a 'eureka' moment. Use AI to audit existing, flawed software tools in your niche. By debugging these tools' pain points with Antigravity and Claude Code, you identify high-demand features that users are desperate to pay for. Transform common software frustrations into your next profitable SaaS project.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the graveyard of ambition. You have the drive to build, but you are paralyzed by the 'no project idea' loop, watching others ship while you analyze endless market trends. The secret isn't a flash of genius; it's spotting the friction in the tools people already use and hate.

By treating existing software as a debugging exercise, you bypass the guesswork. When you peel back the layers of a clunky tool, you find the exact feature gaps that represent immediate, monetizable market needs. It is time to stop searching for an idea and start building the solution to someone else's frustration.

steps.title: Identify the 'Pain' Repository
steps.description: Find an open-source project or a legacy tool in a niche you understand that has open issues or feature requests. This is your goldmine.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this codebase for architectural bottlenecks and common user complaint patterns in the issues tracker. Identify three high-frequency pain points that could be solved with a standalone micro-SaaS.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --analyze-repo ./target-project --focus 'identify common bugs and feature gaps'

steps.title: Simulate the Bug Lifecycle
steps.description: Use AI to recreate the failure states of the target tool. If you can replicate the bug, you can build a better version.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a test suite that reproduces the top three identified bugs in the target software. Explain why these failures occur from an architectural standpoint.

steps.title: Engineer the 'Better' Solution
steps.description: Don't just fix the bug; build the MVP feature that makes the current tool obsolete.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a high-performance alternative implementation for this module that eliminates the current technical debt and adds a feature that users are currently requesting in the forums.

steps.title: Validate with Rapid Prototyping
steps.description: Build a functional prototype that proves your solution works better than the incumbent.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a lean MVP architecture using Next.js and Supabase that solves the pain point we identified. Focus on speed-to-market and essential functionality.

steps.title: Iterative Monetization Loop
steps.description: Package the solution and prepare for user feedback. The goal is to get a 'Yes' from a user before you finish the full build.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a landing page copy and a user-outreach email sequence based on the bug we just solved. Focus on the 'Before vs After' narrative to maximize conversions.

pro_tips: Always target 'mature' projects with high issue counts; the high volume of unresolved bugs is a direct indicator of market opportunity.
Use Claude Code to document your findings as you go; these become your 'case study' marketing content later.
Don't aim for feature parity; aim for feature superiority in the one specific area that causes the most user churn.
Automate your feedback loop by integrating your AI-debugged project with a waitlist tool as early as possible.
Focus on 'boring' B2B problems where users are already accustomed to paying for software, regardless of how clunky it is.
content.next_steps: Once you have validated the bug and built your MVP, push your code to a public repository and start cold-emailing the users who complained about the original tool. Your pitch is simple: 'I solved the bug you reported; want to try the fix?'
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the perfect moment to build. Your next big SaaS project is hidden in the bugs of the tools you use every day. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
